meta.title: Eva-CLI | Multi-Agent Runtime
meta.description: Eva-CLI is an open-source V1.5 source-release CLI for controlled multi-Agent runtimes, combining a Rust boundary, Lua capability layer, Topic EventBus, memory and knowledge context, adapters, hardware plans, and release gates.

home.heroTagline: A controlled runtime for multi-Agent automation
home.heroLead: Eva-CLI uses Rust for reliable runtime boundaries and Lua for hot-reloadable Agent behavior, then connects external Agents, MCP servers, skills, memory, knowledge bases, and hardware through a unified Topic EventBus.

runtimeSteps.label: Rust Core
runtimeSteps.description: Owns policy, memory, recovery, and process boundaries.

runtimeSteps.label: Lua Agents
runtimeSteps.description: Hot-reloads behavior while keeping capability access explicit.

runtimeSteps.label: Adapters
runtimeSteps.description: Routes CLI, MCP, HTTP, skills, and hardware through permissions.

home.runtimePanelNote: V1.5 source-release checks are executable and kept behind controlled runtime boundaries.
home.progressTitle: From architecture scaffold to V1.5 source-release gates
home.progressCurrentBody: The repository now has a compileable Rust workspace, executable CLI commands through release check/security/perf/migration, configuration validation, an in-memory basic loop, task diagnostics, controlled external capability diagnostics, memory context, hardware binding plans, backup and lifecycle checks, CI gates, and release documentation.
progressNextItems: Add signed release artifacts, provenance, and cross-platform installers.
Move destructive restore, release pointer, and supervisor activation paths behind explicit apply gates.
Replace in-memory provider/runtime diagnostics with durable stores and real provider process management.
home.featuredBlogTitle: Latest design notes from the blog
home.featuredBlogBody: Follow the implementation thinking behind Eva-CLI: runtime boundaries, LuaAgent tooling, product direction, and controlled automation design.

progressStages.label: Architecture, module partitioning, and configuration
progressStages.description: System boundaries, runtime model, adapters, memory, discovery, hardware, recovery, website, docs, configuration, and module partitioning are in place.

progressStages.label: Runnable V1.0 runtime and task diagnostics
progressStages.description: The in-memory basic loop, CLI quickstart, local task status/log/cancel diagnostics, and core CI checks are implemented.

progressStages.label: V1.1 to V1.5 controlled capability and release gates
progressStages.description: External capability diagnostics, memory context, hardware plans, backup/lifecycle checks, and release hardening commands are implemented.

progressStages.label: Signed artifacts, installers, and real apply paths
progressStages.description: Future releases add provider process execution, durable stores, destructive apply gates, signed artifacts, and cross-platform installation.

feedback.title: Feedback and suggestions
feedback.body: Send documentation, architecture, release-gate, compatibility, or use-case feedback by email. Eva-CLI is at the V1.5 source-release checkpoint, so concrete reports about command output, docs, migration gaps, and future apply-path contracts are especially useful.
feedback.subjectPlaceholder: For example: add a quick start example
feedback.messagePlaceholder: Describe the suggestion, problem, or missing content.
feedback.note: Submitting opens your local email client and sends the message to iyetmos@gmail.com.
